input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI targeting Android + Windows on Windows 11.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8   # or .9 depending on what's current

# Verify & discover available workloads
dotnet --version
dotnet workload search
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify it's installed
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI/WAP SDK)

```powershell
# Option A: Full Visual Studio (recommended — includes designers + emulators)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then modify the install to add these workloads via CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --wait

# Option B: Just the build tools (no IDE, CI-friendly)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

Visual Studio usually handles this, but to do it manually or verify:

```powershell
# Find where Android SDK landed
$androidHome = if ($env:ANDROID_HOME) { $env:ANDROID_HOME }
               elseif ($env:ANDROID_SDK_ROOT) { $env:ANDROID_SDK_ROOT }
               else { "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" }
Write-Host "Android SDK: $androidHome"

# If SDK is missing, install via command-line tools
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only
# Unzip to $androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\

# Discover available platforms & build tools dynamically
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10

& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -First 10

# Install required components (pick latest versions from above)
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-35" `
    "build-tools;35.0.0" `
    "platform-tools" `
    "emulator" `
    "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Set environment variables persistently
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
    (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" |
     Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName, "User")
```

## 5. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    --name "Pixel_MAUI" `
    --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
    --device "pixel_7"

# Launch it
& "$androidHome\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Run as Administrator
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 7. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiSmokeTest
cd MauiSmokeTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0   # adjust TFM to your SDK

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run

# Discover exact TFMs your template generated
Select-String 'TargetFrameworks' *.csproj
```

## 8. Troubleshooting Commands

```powershell
# Check all installed .NET workloads & their versions
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info

# Repair workloads if something is off
dotnet workload repair

# Check Android SDK components
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Check Hyper-V is enabled (needed for fast Android emulator)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All |
    Select-Object State
# Enable if needed (reboot required):
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

---

**Key version note:** Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` (or whatever `dotnet --version` reports as your major version) throughout. The `Select-String` and `sdkmanager --list` commands let you discover exact versions dynamically rather than hardcoding them.
